ai_strategy.summary: Florida State University has public AI evidence across 4 trend categories, including governance, teaching, research, tools, or workforce activity.

key_provisions: Use reviewed tools for sensitive data.
Follow instructor or unit AI expectations.
Keep human accountability for consequential decisions.
ai_policy.summary: Public sources support a permissive AI posture.

bodies.scope: Institution-level AI coordination evidenced by cited sources.

initiatives.title: AI@FSU initiative launch
initiatives.description: FSU launched AI@FSU as a comprehensive site integrating AI into teaching, learning, research, and campus experience.
initiatives.significance: Shows an R1 institution moving AI into governed practice, research capacity, teaching, or workforce preparation.

investment.description: No public dollar amount found in cited source.

initiatives.policy_implications: Creates or relies on guardrails for privacy, academic integrity, or responsible use.

initiatives.title: Teaching and learning AI guidance
initiatives.description: FSU advises students to check assignment-specific AI rules and to be cautious with data shared to unlicensed AI tools.
initiatives.significance: Shows an R1 institution moving AI into governed practice, research capacity, teaching, or workforce preparation.

investment.description: No public dollar amount found in cited source.

initiatives.policy_implications: Creates or relies on guardrails for privacy, academic integrity, or responsible use.

initiatives.title: Academic integrity AI guide
initiatives.description: FSU faculty materials provide AI academic integrity considerations for assignment design and misconduct evaluation.
initiatives.significance: Shows an R1 institution moving AI into governed practice, research capacity, teaching, or workforce preparation.

investment.description: No public dollar amount found in cited source.

initiatives.title: AI projects and news clearinghouse
initiatives.description: AI@FSU aggregates AI-related projects, news, tools, and examples including faculty and administrator interviews on AI in education.
initiatives.significance: Shows an R1 institution moving AI into governed practice, research capacity, teaching, or workforce preparation.

investment.description: No public dollar amount found in cited source.

initiatives.title: AI research compliance guidance
initiatives.description: FSU Office of Research guidance points researchers to federal AI-related proposal and research integrity expectations.
initiatives.significance: Shows an R1 institution moving AI into governed practice, research capacity, teaching, or workforce preparation.

investment.description: No public dollar amount found in cited source.

initiatives.policy_implications: Creates or relies on guardrails for privacy, academic integrity, or responsible use.

initiatives.title: Microsoft Copilot for the Web for all FSU users
initiatives.description: FSU ITS states Microsoft Copilot for the web is provided securely to all FSU users through FSU login information and is free to students, faculty, and staff.
initiatives.significance: This closes platform usage with named campus-wide access and cost signal.

investment.description: FSU service page lists cost as free.

collaborations.description: Copilot is provided through FSU Microsoft environment.

tools_and_vendors.name: Copilot for the Web
tools_and_vendors.role: secure generative AI assistant for all FSU users

initiatives.policy_implications: Uses FSU login and Microsoft business protections.

initiatives.title: AI Assessment Scale teaching guidance
initiatives.description: FSU’s teaching and learning AI page presents the AI Assessment Scale as a framework for communicating AI usage policies for educational assessments.
initiatives.significance: This closes the assessment-redesign gap with explicit assessment design guidance.
initiatives.policy_implications: Provides a framework for course-level AI use in assessments.

initiatives.title: FSU Research Computing Center GPU resources
initiatives.description: FSU RCC documentation discloses GPU resources totaling 112 GPUs and an HPC service with more than 18,000 cores and more than 700 nodes.
initiatives.significance: This closes compute capacity with named RCC/HPC resources and public GPU count.

investment.description: No total acquisition or operating cost disclosed.

initiatives.title: Ruby AI chatbot for ITS support
initiatives.description: FSU ITS describes Ruby as an AI-driven chatbot connected to the university customer support system that greets users, collects support context, and routes users to support channels.
initiatives.significance: This adds a student/staff-facing AI service signal beyond research and classroom use.

tools_and_vendors.name: Ruby ChatBot
tools_and_vendors.role: AI-driven customer-support intake and routing assistant

research_centers.name: AI research or coordination activity
research_centers.focus: Public AI research, teaching, or tool coordination evidenced in cited sources.

computing_infrastructure.summary: FSU RCC documentation lists GPU resources totaling 112 GPUs across GTX 1080 Ti, RTX A4500 Ada, RTX A4000, RTX A4500, and H100 Tensor Core GPUs, while the service catalog describes more than 18,000 cores and more than 700 nodes.

regulatory_context.summary: State and system AI context checked only at high level; institution-level sources carry the profile.

narrative_summary: Florida State University shows an AI posture with five documented initiatives across 4 categories. The evidence emphasizes AI@FSU initiative launch, Teaching and learning AI guidance, and Academic integrity AI guide. Policy maturity is coded as permissive based on public guidance, privacy language, governance, or tool controls. The profile also records teaching, research, workforce, equity, or enterprise-tool activity where sources support it. Leadership names, enrollment, GPU counts, and contract values are left null when not found in the cited public sources.

gaps_acknowledged: Leadership names and enrollment were not populated in this missing-only sweep.
Detailed AI budgets, user counts, and GPU counts were not consistently public.
No controversy record was added without a reliable institution-specific source.
FSU now has public platform, chatbot, assessment, and GPU-count evidence, but exact Copilot usage counts and M365 Copilot subscription counts remain undisclosed.
Worker 3 did not find public student subgroup outcome metrics for Ruby, Copilot, or AI Assessment Scale adoption.
